result.stdout:
GitHubRelease.Tool:
  Create release notes based on closed GitHub issues and publish GitHub releases.

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool [options] [command]

Options:
  --verbose         Enable verbose output
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  notes      Create release notes
  release    Manage GitHub releases

commands.payload:
GitHubRelease.Tool:
  Create release notes based on closed GitHub issues and publish GitHub releases.

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool [options] [command]

Options:
  --verbose         Enable verbose output
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  notes      Create release notes
  release    Manage GitHub releases

result.stdout:
notes:
  Create release notes

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool notes [options]

Options:
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>            The root directory of the git repository [default: .]
  --header <header>                                An optional header of the release notes
  -f, --format <Markdown|PlainText>                The output format of the release notes [default: Markdown]
  -o, --output-file <output-file>                  The output file path. If not specified, the release notes are written to STDOUT
  -m, --output-mode <Append|Overwrite|Prepend>     Whether output-file should be overwritten, appended or prepended to [default: Overwrite]
  -e, --output-encoding <output-encoding>          The encoding to use when writing release notes to a file [default: utf-8]
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                The token used for GitHub API authentication
  -g, --git-tag-regex <git-tag-regex>              A regex to use when looking for the git tag representing the previous release. If not specified, the most recent tag is considered as the previous release
  -c, --configuration-file <configuration-file>    The release notes configuration file to use. If not specified, the repository root directory and current directory are searched to find one
  --verbose                                        Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                   Show help and usage information

notes:

Usage:

Options:

  --header <header>                                An optional header of the release notes
  -f, --format <Markdown|PlainText>                The output format of the release notes [default: Markdown]
  -o, --output-file <output-file>                  The output file path. If not specified, the release notes are written to STDOUT
  -m, --output-mode <Append|Overwrite|Prepend>     Whether output-file should be overwritten, appended or prepended to [default: Overwrite]
  -e, --output-encoding <output-encoding>          The encoding to use when writing release notes to a file [default: utf-8]
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                The token used for GitHub API authentication
  -g, --git-tag-regex <git-tag-regex>              A regex to use when looking for the git tag representing the previous release. If not specified, the most recent tag is considered as the previous release
  -c, --configuration-file <configuration-file>    The release notes configuration file to use. If not specified, the repository root directory and current directory are searched to find one
  --verbose                                        Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
release:
  Manage GitHub releases

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release [options] [command]

Options:
  --verbose         Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create    Create a GitHub release
  get       Get GitHub releases
  update    Update a GitHub release
  delete    Delete a GitHub release

commands.payload:
release:
  Manage GitHub releases

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release [options] [command]

Options:
  --verbose         Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create    Create a GitHub release
  get       Get GitHub releases
  update    Update a GitHub release
  delete    Delete a GitHub release

result.stdout:
create:
  Create a GitHub release

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release create [options]

Options:
  -n, --name <name>                                  An optional name of the release
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                       The tag name of the release
  --target, --target-commitish <target-commitish>    A commitish value to specify the target of the tag. Defaults to the repository's default branch. Unused if the tag already exists
  --body <body>                                      A file containing the body of the release (usually a markdown file)
  --prerelease                                       Whether or not the release is a pre-release
  --draft                                            Whether or not the release is in draft state. To publish the release use '--draft false' [default: True]
  --assets <assets>                                  A collection of assets to include in the release
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                  The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>     The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>        The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>              The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                          Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
create:
  Create a GitHub release

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release create [options]

Options:
  -n, --name <name>                                  An optional name of the release
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                       The tag name of the release
  --target, --target-commitish <target-commitish>    A commitish value to specify the target of the tag. Defaults to the repository's default branch. Unused if the tag already exists
  --body <body>                                      A file containing the body of the release (usually a markdown file)
  --prerelease                                       Whether or not the release is a pre-release
  --draft                                            Whether or not the release is in draft state. To publish the release use '--draft false' [default: True]
  --assets <assets>                                  A collection of assets to include in the release
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                  The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>     The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>        The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>              The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                          Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
delete:
  Delete a GitHub release

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release delete [options]

Options:
  --id <id>                                         Delete release by ID
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                      Delete release by tag name
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                 The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>    The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>       The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>             The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                         Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
get:
  Get GitHub releases

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release get [options]

Options:
  --id <id>                                         Get release by ID
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                      Get release by tag name
  --all                                             Gets all releases
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                 The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>    The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>       The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>             The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                         Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                    Show help and usage information

get:

Usage:

Options:
  --id <id>                                         Get release by ID
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                      Get release by tag name
  --all                                             Gets all releases
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                 The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>    The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>       The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>             The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                         Enable verbose output

result.stdout:
update:
  Update a GitHub release

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release update [options]

Options:
  --id <id>                                          The ID of the release to update
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                       The current tag of the release. Can be used instead of '--id' to identify the release to update. Requires the tag to exist in the GitHub repository
  -n, --name <name>                                  The new name of the release
  --new-tag, --new-tag-name <new-tag-name>           The new tag name of the release
  --target, --target-commitish <target-commitish>    The new commitish value to specify the target of the tag. Defaults to the repository's default branch. Unused if the tag already exists
  --body <body>                                      A file containing the new body of the release
  --prerelease                                       Whether or not the release is a pre-release
  --draft                                            Whether or not the release is in draft state. To publish the release use '--draft false'
  --assets <assets>                                  A collection of assets to include in the updated release
  --clear-assets                                     Whether or not to delete all the existing assets
  --overwrite-assets                                 Whether or not to overwrite assets that match the name of assets in this update. Only has an effect if this update has any assets and '--clear-assets' is not specified. To disable overwriting of assets, use '--overwrite-assets false' [default: True]
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                  The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>     The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>        The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>              The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                          Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
update:
  Update a GitHub release

Usage:
  GitHubRelease.Tool release update [options]

Options:
  --id <id>                                          The ID of the release to update
  --tag, --tag-name <tag-name>                       The current tag of the release. Can be used instead of '--id' to identify the release to update. Requires the tag to exist in the GitHub repository
  -n, --name <name>                                  The new name of the release
  --new-tag, --new-tag-name <new-tag-name>           The new tag name of the release
  --target, --target-commitish <target-commitish>    The new commitish value to specify the target of the tag. Defaults to the repository's default branch. Unused if the tag already exists
  --body <body>                                      A file containing the new body of the release
  --prerelease                                       Whether or not the release is a pre-release
  --draft                                            Whether or not the release is in draft state. To publish the release use '--draft false'
  --assets <assets>                                  A collection of assets to include in the updated release
  --clear-assets                                     Whether or not to delete all the existing assets
  --overwrite-assets                                 Whether or not to overwrite assets that match the name of assets in this update. Only has an effect if this update has any assets and '--clear-assets' is not specified. To disable overwriting of assets, use '--overwrite-assets false' [default: True]
  -t, --github-token <github-token>                  The token used for GitHub API authentication
  --owner, --repository-owner <repository-owner>     The name of the GitHub organization or user owning the target repository
  --repo, --repository-name <repository-name>        The name of the target repository
  -r, --repository-dir <repository-dir>              The root directory of the git repository. Alternative of --repository-owner and --repository-name. Determines target repository owner and name by the remotes of the local git repository
  --verbose                                          Enable verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                                     Show help and usage information
